primary_generic_source: Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide (canonical text supplied by user, matching https://humble.fish/community/threads/medication-dosing-guide.1124/)
notes: All calculated_brand_mg_per_gal_to_deliver_pure_dose values are based on active_ingredient_pct and the generic LOW dose. Measure weights are left null when not published on the label; this is tracked in the verification log. | v1.1 (2026-04-18): Added MetroPlex in-house scoop weight (150 mg). Added fishotel_default overrides for Copper Power (1.73 ml/gal empirical) and Cupramine (HF ramp). Triple Sulfa substitution surfaced via ui_user_surface_note. Hyposalinity duration 5→30 days. Added sulfa_tmp, minocycline, fenbendazole_internal as meds 23-25. | v1.2 (2026-04-18): Jeff-supplied updates from Fritz/API/Seachem/Copper Power/PolyGuard data dump. Filled measure_weight_mg for NeoPlex (125), KanaPlex (125), SulfaPlex (145), Fritz Maracyn (600), Maracyn Two (600). Confirmed API packets all 2000 mg total weight. Added Copper Power 0.5% Cu(II) and SG 1.12. Added Seachem PolyGuard (med 26) with full 5-active composition disclosure. | v1.3 (2026-04-18): Added bath_protocol framework. Schema additions: treatment_type on every med ('prolonged' | 'bath' | 'both'); bath_protocol nested object on the 8 bath-capable meds (ciprofloxacin, enrofloxacin, fenbendazole (external), formalin_37, hydrogen_peroxide_3, methylene_blue, acriflavine, nitrofuracin_green); temperature_contraindication_celsius=27 on formalin_37 with amber-warning text; root-level default_bath_safety object carrying Noga's universal abort criteria, aeration requirement, default scaleless-fish sensitivity, and bioassay recommendation. Enrofloxacin and Nitrofuracin Green implement Standard/Aggressive tiers per Jeff's discrepancy adjudications. All new data points cite Noga (2nd ed., 2010) by chapter, entry, book page, and PDF page; all Noga material is paraphrased, never quoted. See discrepancy-report.md for the four adjudicated Humblefish-vs-Noga conflicts and the ciprofloxacin/acriflavine Noga gaps.

abort_criteria.description: Observable signs that require immediate termination of a bath and transfer of the fish to clean, untreated, aerated water — even if the scheduled bath time is incomplete. Applies to every bath_protocol in this schema unless that protocol explicitly overrides a specific field.
signs: Attempts to jump out of the bath container
Depression (unusual listlessness, sinking to the bottom)
Loss of equilibrium (inability to stay upright)
Listing to one side (tilting rather than swimming level)
abort_criteria.immediate_action: Net the fish out of the bath immediately and return to clean, untreated, well-aerated water. Do not wait to see whether the fish recovers in the treatment container.
abort_criteria.followup: After aborting or completing any bath, observe the fish closely over the next several days before deciding whether a second session is indicated.
abort_criteria.source_note: Noga also notes that bath toxicity is most common when antiseptic-class drugs are used (formalin, hydrogen peroxide, methylene blue, acriflavine, nitrofurans).
abort_criteria.verification_source_citation: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010. Chapter 16 'General Concepts in Therapy', 'Bath Method in a Small Volume of Water' framework, Sample Calculation step 3. Book p.362 (PDF p.381).

aeration_requirement.description: Vigorous aeration of the treatment water is mandatory for any bath lasting longer than one minute.
aeration_requirement.rationale: Concentrated treatment water in a small bath container rapidly depletes dissolved oxygen, and several common bath drugs (formalin, methylene blue) either consume oxygen directly or impair gill function. Without active aeration, hypoxic stress compounds drug toxicity.
aeration_requirement.verification_source_citation: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010. Chapter 16 'General Concepts in Therapy', 'Bath Method in a Small Volume of Water', step 3. Book p.362 (PDF p.381).

default_species_sensitivity.description: Species-sensitivity baseline inherited by every bath_protocol unless the specific medication's bath_protocol sets `suppress_default_scaleless_sensitivity: true` with a justifying source note.
default_species_sensitivity.scaleless_species_warning: Scaleless and partially-scaled species (catfish, loaches, eels, many moray varieties, and some wrasses) are consistently more sensitive to water-borne treatments than fully-scaled species. For any untested species, bioassay a single individual before treating a group.
default_species_sensitivity.verification_source_citation: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010. Chapter 16 'General Concepts in Therapy', introductory material on water-borne treatments. Book p.358 (PDF p.377). Reinforced in the Nitrofurazone entry at book p.381 (PDF p.400) and in several other drug-specific entries.

bioassay_recommendation.description: Before treating any group of fish with a medication whose species-specific response is unknown, test the protocol on a small number of individuals first. Even well-established doses can behave unexpectedly in a new system because temperature, hardness, and pH strongly modulate drug toxicity.
bioassay_recommendation.verification_source_citation: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010. Chapter 16 'General Concepts in Therapy'. Book p.361–362 (PDF p.380–381).

medications.special_notes_generic: Applied as a 1-2 hour bath at 250 mg per gallon, repeated daily. Methylene Blue can be added to the bath water to boost efficacy. Not typically used as an in-tank QT dose.

tiers.recovery_instructions: After each bath, return the fish to clean untreated seawater. Observe for several days after the final session before deciding on any further course.
tiers.indication: Bacterial infections of marine fish, particularly resistant gram-negative cultures. Methylene blue may be added to the bath water to boost efficacy.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Dose and schedule: Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide and the Humblefish Ciprofloxacin thread. Noga (2nd ed.) does not cover ciprofloxacin in its fluoroquinolone section — enrofloxacin metabolizes to ciprofloxacin in some species but cipro is not independently profiled. No corroborating Noga data is available for this medication; Humblefish stands alone.

bath_protocol.calendar_event_note: 7 consecutive daily bath sessions, ~60–120 min each

medications.special_notes_generic: Can be given in-tank as a QT dose, or as a 5-hour bath. Prefer the low end of the range for sensitive species. In vivo it partially de-ethylates to ciprofloxacin, so it has overlapping coverage.

tiers.recovery_instructions: After the 5-hour bath, return the fish to clean untreated seawater and observe over several days before deciding on any follow-up course.
tiers.indication: Bacterial infections responsive to fluoroquinolones. Prefer the low end of the concentration range for sensitive species (anthias, dragonets, wrasses).
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: Anthias, dragonets, and wrasses flagged as sensitive per the top-level sensitivity_species array on this med.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide: 9.5–19 mg/gal, single ~5-hour bath session. Concentration range matches the pure-dose range already recorded in the prolonged-dose fields of this med at v1.2.
tiers.tier_note: Standard tier: a single one-off ~5-hour bath. Lower logistical burden than the Aggressive tier.

tiers.repeat_schedule: Continuous therapeutic immersion for 5 days with a 50–75% water change between daily dose intervals.
tiers.recovery_instructions: At the end of the 5-day course, move the fish to a fully untreated observation system. Watch closely for any late-onset adverse signs.
tiers.indication: More severe or refractory bacterial infections where a single short bath is unlikely to achieve therapeutic clearance.
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: A 5-day therapeutic immersion is a much heavier exposure than a single bath. Anthias, dragonets, and wrasses may not tolerate it. Bioassay a single individual first for any untested species.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Enrofloxacin (Baytril) entry, water-borne formulations, 'Bath' formulation 1a: 2.5–5 mg/L (9.5–19 mg/gal), 5 days, with 50–75% water change between treatments. Cites Lewbart et al. 1997. Book p.378 (PDF p.397). Noga labels this a 'Bath' in his taxonomy, but functionally it is a multi-day therapeutic immersion rather than a short dip — see discrepancy-report.md item #1 for the adjudication.
tiers.tier_note: Aggressive tier: 5-day therapeutic immersion. Use when the Standard-tier single session is clinically insufficient. Do not stack with other antibiotics without veterinary input.

bath_protocol.calendar_event_note: Standard: one 5-hour session. Aggressive: 5 consecutive days with water-change checkpoints.

medications.special_notes_generic: Humblefish frames the 12.5-25 mg/gal dose around eye infections; treat 10-14 days total. Pharma-grade powder (e.g. from Everything Aquatic) is preferred over aquarium brands because fillers are lower. Remove chemical filtration and turn off UV while dosing.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: Weigh with a digital scale to deliver 12.5-25 mg/gal of pure erythromycin
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Humblefish-recommended source for pharma-grade erythromycin because of the high purity.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 packet (200 mg erythromycin) per 10 US gallons
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Humblefish describes Fritz Maracyn as <35% erythromycin; DailyMed lists 200 mg active per packet. Packet powder weight (571 mg) is back-calculated from that active mass at 35% potency and should be treated as approximate - flagged in verification_log. Label schedule (1 packet/10 gal × 5 days) delivers ~20 mg/gal per dose, which is inside the Humblefish 12.5-25 mg/gal range.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: fda.report/DailyMed Maracyn monograph: 0.6 g per packet (200 mg erythromycin)

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 packet (200 mg erythromycin) per 10 US gallons
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Humblefish describes API E.M. as <10% erythromycin. DailyMed confirms 200 mg active per 2 g packet. Label is a 2-dose course with 25% water change between, not a 10-day QT course.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: DailyMed label: 200 mg erythromycin in 2 g packet (confirmed via uploaded PDF 2026-04-18)

medications.special_notes_generic: Dose every 48 h (drop to every 24 h for severe infections) for 10-14 days. Most effective when paired with nitrofurazone (e.g. AAP Spectrogram delivers both in one capsule). Remove carbon, switch off UV during treatment.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 measure per 20 L (5 US gallons)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DailyMed confirms 32% kanamycin. Measure weight 125 mg → 40 mg kanamycin per measure. DOSING DISCREPANCY: Seachem label (1 measure / 5 gal = 8 mg/gal kanamycin) is roughly 3× below Humblefish therapeutic range (25-37.5 mg/gal). To hit HF therapeutic, dose 6.25 measures per 10 gal, not the label 2 measures per 10 gal.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: Seachem FAQ explicitly: 125 mg per measure (0.25 mL scoop)

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 capsule per 10 US gallons
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Kanamycin + nitrofurazone combination capsule. Active percentages not publicly published. Widely regarded as strongest broad-spectrum aquarium antibiotic for columnaris, aeromonas, pseudomonas. Total actives ~150 mg per capsule per forum sources (unofficial — AAP received FDA warning letters for unapproved labeling, hence no DailyMed entry). Dosing: 1 capsule / 10 gal.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: Forum reference: ~150 mg total actives per capsule (UNOFFICIAL — AAP does not publish)

medications.special_notes_generic: Treat 10 days for common bacterial infection. For suspected fish tuberculosis (Mycobacterium), extend to 30 days. 25% WC before each dose, remove carbon, UV off.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 measure per 8 L (2 US gallons)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DailyMed confirms 43% neomycin sulfate. Seachem's label protocol (weekly for 3 weeks) is very different from the Humblefish daily QT protocol - capture both schedules. Measure mg weight not published.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: Seachem FAQ: 10 g vial ≈ 80 measures → 125 mg/measure (identical 0.25 mL scoop across products)

medications.special_notes_generic: Combination product - nitrofurazone + sulfathiazole sodium + methylene blue on a NaCl carrier. Humblefish in-tank dose: 1/4 tsp per 20 gal = 1 g per 20 gal = 50 mg/gal. Bath-only dose: 100 mg/gal for 30 min.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1/4 teaspoon per 20 US gallons (~1 g per 20 gallons)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Active percentages not published by AAP. Hobbyist reverse-engineering suggests ~25 mg/gal nitrofurazone + ~25 mg/gal sulfathiazole + ~0.5 ml/gal methylene blue at the label dose (not manufacturer-confirmed - flagged). 4 g/tsp is a standard salt-like powder density, treated as approximate.

tiers.repeat_schedule: Single session; repeat only after clinical reassessment.
tiers.recovery_instructions: Return the fish to clean untreated seawater after 30 minutes. Water will be stained yellow-green from the methylene blue component and tinted brown from the nitrofurazone component; neither is cause for concern.
tiers.indication: Routine bath dip for suspected bacterial and superficial parasitic infections on marine fish as part of a QT intake workflow.
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: Scaleless species are specifically flagged by Noga as more sensitive to water-borne nitrofurans.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide, Humblefish NFG-specific threads, and empirical validation by @Dierks at FisHotel. Scaleless-fish sensitivity, photosensitivity, and nitrofuran bacterial-resistance profile paraphrased from Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Nifurpirinol and Nitrofurazone entries, book p.381 (PDF p.400).
tiers.tier_note: Jeff's empirical standard, used as routine intake QT at FisHotel.

tiers.repeat_schedule: Single session. Re-evaluate clinically before any repeat.
tiers.recovery_instructions: Move the fish to clean, fully-aerated untreated seawater immediately at the end of the 30 minutes and observe constantly for at least another 30 minutes for any delayed toxicity signs.
tiers.heightened_warning: This concentration is approximately 3.8× the Standard tier (~4× informally). Reserve for severe or life-threatening bacterial presentations where the Standard tier has failed or is clearly insufficient. Lower abort threshold — any early distress signs mean end the bath immediately.
tiers.indication: Severe bacterial infections on marine fish where a shorter high-concentration exposure is clinically indicated.
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: At this concentration, scaleless and sensitive species (anthias, dragonets, wrasses) should be routed to the Standard tier instead, or approached with extra caution.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Nitrofurazone (Furacyn) entry, water-borne formulations, 'Bath' formulation 1a: 100 mg/L (380 mg/gal), 30 minutes. Book p.381 (PDF p.400). Nitrofuracin Green is a branded combination product (nitrofurazone + sulfathiazole sodium + methylene blue on a NaCl carrier) and is not in Noga — nitrofurazone is its principal antibacterial active and is used here as the closest available proxy. See discrepancy-report.md item #4.
tiers.tier_note: Approximately 3.8× the Standard tier. Reserved for severe presentations; uses the same pattern as the Copper sensitivity toggle in the calculator UI.

bath_protocol.calendar_event_note: Single-session bath at either tier; no .ics export

medications.special_notes_generic: Every 48 h standard, drop to every 24 h for severe infections. 25-50% water change before each dose (50% recommended by Humblefish for heavier dose schedules). 10-14 days total. Synergistic with kanamycin. Light-sensitive - ideally dose with tank lights off. Stains silicone yellow.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 packet (85 mg nitrofurazone) per 10 US gallons
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DailyMed confirms 85 mg nitrofurazone per packet (delivers 8.5 mg/gal at label dose - just below the Humblefish range). Total packet weight and excipient percentages not published. Furan-2 label is a 4-day course.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: DailyMed label: 85 mg nitrofurazone in 2 g packet (same 2g format as API E.M./General Cure)

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 capsule per 10 US gallons
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Same product as for kanamycin - delivers both actives synchronously.

medications.special_notes_generic: Humblefish generic dose refers to a combined sulfa mix (sulfamerazine + sulfamethazine + sulfathiazole) at 25 mg/gal total. Drop to every 24 h for severe infections. Minimum 10 days. Note: the most common aquarium brand (API Triple Sulfa) actually substitutes sulfacetamide for sulfamerazine.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 packet per 10 US gallons (delivers 664 mg sulfathiazole Na + 168 mg sulfamethazine Na + 168 mg sulfacetamide Na per 10 gal)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Substitutes sulfacetamide for sulfamerazine vs. the HF generic recipe. Total label dose delivers ~100 mg/gal combined sulfa sodium salts. Packet total weight ~1 g (inferred, not manufacturer-confirmed).
brand_equivalents.ui_user_surface_note: Contains sulfacetamide sodium in place of sulfamerazine — different pharmacokinetics than the generic HF sulfa recipe.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: API SDS: 664 sulfathiazole Na + 168 sulfamethazine Na + 168 sulfacetamide Na = 1000 mg actives in 2 g packet

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 2-3 measures per 40 L (10 US gallons)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DailyMed confirms 69% sulfathiazole. Measure weight 145 mg (total powder) → 100 mg sulfathiazole active per measure. Label "2-3 measures per 10 gal" matches HF 25 mg/gal at 2.5 measures/10 gal.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: Seachem support: ~145 mg per measure (69% sulfathiazole = ~100 mg active per measure)

dispensing_formula.source_note: FisHotel Hanna-verified 1.73 ml/gal at 2.5 ppm. Manufacturer label 1.48 ml/gal under-doses ~15-20%.

medications.special_notes_generic: Target 2.2-2.5 ppm total copper. Ramp over 4-5 days; never hit therapeutic in a single dose. Remove all inverts, carbon, Purigen, and chemical filtration. Turn off UV. Test with Hanna HI702 High Range Copper Checker - Seachem/Salifert kits are not appropriate for this product.

brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DATA CONFLICT: Label/calculator value is 1.48 ml/gal for 2.5 ppm. Empirical Hanna HI702 testing by multiple hobbyists lands at ~1.73 ml/gal to actually reach 2.5 ppm. Treat 1.73 ml/gal as the conservative empirical value and ALWAYS verify with the Hanna checker. Never trust the label dose blindly. Technical data sheet confirms 0.5% Cu(II) w/w, SG 1.12. Math check: 1.73 ml × 1.12 g/ml × 0.005 = 9.69 mg Cu in 1 gal (3.785 L) = 2.56 ppm — matches FisHotel empirical.

fishotel_default.dose: 1.73 ml per gallon to reach 2.5 ppm
fishotel_default.override_reason: Manufacturer label value (1.48 ml/gal) under-doses by ~15-20% when verified against a Hanna HI702 checker. FisHotel-tested dosing.

brand_equivalents.manufacturer_label_dose: 1.48 ml per gallon to reach 2.5 ppm (manufacturer label / common calculator value)

dispensing_formula.source_note: Copper sulfate pentahydrate is 25.5% free copper by weight. Extremely narrow therapeutic window — test with Seachem or Salifert copper kit before every adjustment. Dissolve thoroughly before adding.

medications.special_notes_generic: Free Cu2+ target 0.15-0.20 ppm. Copper sulfate pentahydrate is 25.5% free copper by weight. Ramp over 4-5 days. Extremely narrow therapeutic window - test with a Seachem or Salifert copper kit before every adjustment. Remove inverts and chemical filtration. UV off.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: ~0.22 g of pentahydrate salt per 100 gal to reach 0.15 ppm Cu2+ (UF/IFAS FA165 formula)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Peer-reviewed UF/IFAS aquaculture guide; formula: gallons × 0.0038 × desired mg/L × 3.92 = grams of pentahydrate needed. Worked value: ~2.22 mg of pentahydrate powder per gallon delivers ~0.15 ppm Cu2+. The standard calculated_brand_mg_per_gal formula (dose_low ÷ active_pct) does not apply cleanly here because the pure dose is expressed as ppm of Cu2+ (mg/L) not mg/gal of compound - left null to avoid unit confusion downstream.

dispensing_formula.source_note: Seachem Cupramine: 1 ml per 5 gallons brings tank to 0.5 ppm. Alternative: 4 drops per gallon.

medications.special_notes_generic: Target 0.50 ppm total copper. Humblefish-preferred ramp dose is smaller than Seachem's label (Humblefish: 4 drops (0.2 ml) per gallon OR 1 ml per 5 gallons; Seachem label: 1 ml per 10.5 gal first dose, 48 h later second dose to 0.5 ppm). Inverts removed, carbon and Purigen out, UV off. Test with Seachem MultiTest or Salifert. Do not exceed 0.6 ppm.

brand_equivalents.brand_notes: HF ramped protocol: 4 drops (0.2 ml) per gal OR 1 ml per 5 gal. Seachem label protocol (two-step 48 h) is faster than HF recommends. Cupramine is a copper-amine complex, not chelated - it stays ionic in the water column. Remove with carbon or CupriSorb after treatment.

fishotel_default.dose: 0.2 ml (4 drops) per gallon, ramped over multiple days to therapeutic 0.5 ppm
fishotel_default.override_reason: HF's slower ramp is safer for sensitivity-flagged species (anthias, dragonets, wrasses, scaleless fish).

brand_equivalents.manufacturer_label_dose: 1 ml per 40 L (~10.5 gal) day 1; wait 48 h; repeat to reach 0.5 ppm

dispensing_formula.source_note: Ruby Reef/Brightwell Cuprion: 1 drop per gallon (~0.05 ml) brings tank to 0.2 ppm.

medications.special_notes_generic: Target 0.20 ppm ionic copper. Very tight therapeutic window - above ~0.25 ppm turns toxic quickly. Daily testing required (Seachem or Salifert). Not reef-safe. For experienced aquarists only.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 drop per gallon raises free copper by ~0.20 ppm
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Ruby Reef brand originally; now sold under Brightwell Aquatics. Drop volume assumed ~0.05 ml (standard).

medications.name_generic: Coppersafe / Kordon Copper Aid (chelated copper, 2.0 ppm target)

dispensing_formula.source_note: Fritz/Mardel Coppersafe: 1 tsp per 4 gal brings tank to 2.0 ppm chelated copper. Verify with Hanna HI702.

medications.special_notes_generic: Chelated coppers hold dosed level for roughly a month without redosing. Test with Hanna HI702 High Range Copper Checker - Seachem/Salifert don't read chelated copper correctly. Ramp gently even though the chemistry is more forgiving than ionic copper. Remove inverts, carbon, Purigen, and UV.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 5 ml (1 tsp) per 4 US gal (or 8 oz / 190 US gal) to reach ~2.0 ppm total copper
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Chelated. Stays active ~1 month. Always verify with Hanna HI702 - hobbyists report label dose usually under-shoots 2.0 ppm.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 tsp (5 ml) per 4 US gallons
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Chelated copper sulfate. Multiple hobbyist reports that the label dose delivers notably below 2.0 ppm; plan to overshoot against the Hanna checker.

medications.special_notes_generic: 12-hour bath at 25 mg/L (~95 mg/gal) for flukes and external flatworms. Not easily water-soluble - dissolve first in DMSO at 10 mg fenbendazole per 1 ml DMSO. The bath must be repeated roughly 1 week later to catch newly-hatched worms; timing via the BeNeZe/Cawthron 2022 calendar. Fenbendazole is toxic to many invertebrates; not reef safe.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: Hobbyist use: weigh 430 mg Panacur C (yields ~95 mg fenbendazole) per gallon for a 12-hour external bath
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Off-label aquarium use. 1 g granule packets labeled for dogs. Always weigh on a mg scale rather than trusting scoops.

tiers.repeat_schedule: Repeat the 12-hour bath once, approximately 7 days after the first session, to catch worms that hatched from eggs the first bath did not kill.
tiers.recovery_instructions: At the end of the 12-hour bath, return the fish to clean untreated seawater. Observe over the next several days. Schedule the second session one week later per the standard fluke life-cycle calendar.
tiers.indication: External monogenean flukes and flatworms on marine fish.
tiers.solubility_note: Fenbendazole is not readily water-soluble. Pre-dissolve in DMSO at 10 mg fenbendazole per 1 ml DMSO, or use weighed Panacur C granules via the brand substitution already in this record.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Fenbendazole (Panacur) entry, Use No. 2 'Treatment of monogeneans', water-borne formulation 1a: 25 mg/L (≈95 mg/gal) for 12 hours. Book p.398 (PDF p.417). Concentration and duration match the Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide exactly; the 7-day repeat cadence is Humblefish-derived but is consistent with Noga's weekly-for-three-weeks prolonged-immersion nematode schedule.

bath_protocol.calendar_event_note: Two 12-hour bath sessions: Day 1 and Day 8

medications.special_notes_generic: Humblefish QT dose: 9.5 mg/gal (2.5 mg/L). Not water-soluble - pre-dissolve in a few drops of vodka (or DMSO at 1 ml per 20 mg) before adding. Must dose AT LEAST twice, roughly 1 week apart, to catch newly-hatched flukes - use the BeNeZe/Cawthron 2022 calendar. Broadly toxic to inverts and snails. Keep temp stable during the protocol.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 tsp (~5 ml) per 20 US gallons to reach ~2.5 mg/L praziquantel
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Label concentration is <5% praziquantel by weight. Already pre-solubilized - no DMSO/vodka needed. Repeat no more than every 3 days; a single treatment lasts 5-7 days.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 packet per 10 US gallons delivers 250 mg metronidazole + 75 mg praziquantel per 10 gal
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DailyMed confirms the 250/75 mg split. Single packet-dose only delivers 7.5 mg/gal praziquantel (just below HF's 9.5 mg/gal target), so most QT operators round up. Not reef-safe - snail/shrimp/crab toxic. Second dose 48 h later per label; HF prefers week-spaced redosing for full life-cycle coverage.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: DailyMed label: 75 mg prazi + 250 mg metro in 2 g packet

medications.special_notes_generic: NOT A MEDICATION - a salinity protocol. Hold at 15 ppt / 1.011 SG for 5 days to eliminate juvenile and adult flukes plus their eggs. Drop salinity over 48 h, hold for 5 days, then ramp back up over several days. Not reef-safe; kills inverts. Incompatible with sharks, rays, and other osmotically-sensitive species. Full protocol including ramp-down and ramp-up typically runs 4-6 weeks; 30 days is the minimum at target salinity. Some operators hold 72+ days for persistent Neobenedenia infestations.

medications.source_note: No in-tank prolonged-immersion protocol is recommended for Ruby Reef Rally at this concentration — Rally is a bath-only medication (1 tsp per gallon, 90-minute bath). For prolonged immersion, Ruby Reef Rally Pro is the separate product intended for display tanks (1 oz per 10 gal daily for 3+ days per the brand notes). Switch to Bath mode above to dose.
medications.special_notes_generic: Humblefish recommends using Ruby Reef Rally rather than raw acriflavine powder. Bath protocol: 1 tsp Rally per gallon for 90 minutes. Stains silicone and rockwork yellow-green. Photolabile. Not reef-safe.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 tsp per gallon as a 90-minute bath (Humblefish protocol)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Three-active liquid: Acriflavine HCl + Aminoacridine HCl + Formalin (trace activator). Exact percentages proprietary. Per Ruby Reef: sequential reagent blending with formalin reduces required acriflavine/aminoacridine by 10× vs dosing separately. Rally Pro: 1 oz (30 mL) per 10 gal saltwater, daily × 3+ days (skimmer/carbon off). Reef-safe, invertebrate-safe, biofilter-safe, copper-free. Turns water yellow-green temporarily.

tiers.concentration_unit: tsp of Ruby Reef Rally per gal
tiers.repeat_schedule: Single session for the short bath. Optionally followed by Rally Pro prolonged immersion (1 oz per 10 gal daily for 3+ days) per the existing brand notes on this med.
tiers.recovery_instructions: Return the fish to clean untreated seawater at the end of the 90 minutes. The treatment water stains yellow-green; this is cosmetic and does not indicate active medication.
tiers.indication: Bacterial and parasitic infections on marine fish, including Cryptocaryon irritans, velvet, and Uronema.
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: Acriflavine is a photosensitizer. Keep the bath container shielded from bright light, and if any follow-up prolonged immersion is used, keep the display dim.
tiers.source_note: Noga (Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Acriflavin entry, book p.376, PDF p.395) describes the agent but does NOT provide a specific bath concentration. Noga is skeptical of acriflavine's clinical value due to widespread bacterial resistance and the availability of more effective alternatives. Noga also flags that high acriflavine doses may inhibit normal swim-bladder inflation in developing fry (Sanabria et al. 2009). See discrepancy-report.md.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Dose per Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide and the Humblefish Acriflavine / Ruby Reef Rally thread: 1 tsp Rally per gallon, 90 minutes. Noga consulted for safety context only; does not provide a numeric bath concentration.

bath_protocol.calendar_event_note: 90-minute bath is typically one-off; Rally Pro daily immersion, if used, can be manually scheduled.

medications.special_notes_generic: 40-60 mg/gal, 'one-and-done' dosing in a bare-bottom QT with no biological filtration (biofilters degrade CP). Treat 30 days, or 14 days followed by transfer to a sterile observation tank at least 10 ft away. Use for ich, velvet, uronema, brook. Do not use carbon or UV. No reliable hobbyist test kit.

medications.source_note: In-tank (bare QT) protocol: 1 ml of 37% formalin per 10 gal (= 0.1 ml/gal) once daily for up to 11 days, preceded by a 50% water change before each dose. Aerate heavily — formalin depletes dissolved oxygen. Not reef-safe. Source: Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide, Formalin entry (canonical text).
medications.special_notes_generic: 37% formaldehyde solution. In-tank (bare QT): 1 ml per 10 gal, or 2 drops per gallon daily. Bath: 30-60 min at 0.6 ml/gal (~12 drops/gal). Depletes oxygen - aerate heavily during any bath. Carcinogen - wear gloves, ventilate. Not reef-safe.
medications.temperature_contraindication_note: Formalin is contraindicated above 27°C (80.6°F) — oxygen demand rises and toxicity increases. Cool your bath water or choose an alternative treatment.
medications.temperature_contraindication_source_cita: Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Formalin entry, Use No. 1, contraindication text. Book p.399–400 (PDF p.418–419).

tiers.repeat_schedule: May be repeated 2–3 times, once daily, if clinically needed. Noga restricts the maximum concentration to once every 3 days when water is warm.
tiers.recovery_instructions: Return the fish to clean, well-aerated water immediately at the end of the bath. Observe closely for 24–72 hours. Idiosyncratic late-onset mortality has been reported up to 72 hours post-treatment; typical toxicity signs are piping at the surface, excess mucus production, and pale coloration.
tiers.aeration_note: Vigorous aeration is mandatory. Every 5 mg/L of formalin removes roughly 1 mg/L of dissolved oxygen from the bath water.
tiers.indication: External protozoan and metazoan ectoparasites including marine ich and monogenean flukes.
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: Elasmobranchs (sharks, rays) are especially sensitive — bioassay required before treating any untested species. Do not use on fish that were recently stressed (shipped, transported, netted repeatedly) or that have open skin ulcers.
tiers.temperature_warning: Above 21°C (70°F) for warmwater species, or above 10°C (50°F) for coldwater species, do not exceed 0.63 ml/gal. Above 27°C (80°F), formalin is contraindicated entirely — see temperature_contraindication_celsius on the parent med.
contraindications: Fish recently stressed (shipped, transported, or netted repeatedly)
Soft, acidic water (toxicity is elevated in low alkalinity)
tiers.verification_source_citation: Core dose (0.6 ml/gal, 30–60 min) per Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide. Dose range, temperature caps, contraindications, and late-onset toxicity signs paraphrased from Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Formalin entry, Use No. 1 'Treatment of protozoan and metazoan ectoparasites', water-borne formulation 1a: 125–250 ppm (0.47–0.95 ml/gal) for up to 60 minutes. Book p.399–400 (PDF p.418–419).

bath_protocol.calendar_event_note: Typically a one-off short bath; no .ics export. If repeating 2–3 daily sessions, Jeff can manually add calendar events.

medications.special_notes_generic: Standard dip: 20 ml of 3% H2O2 per gallon of saltwater for 30 minutes. Sensitive species should be monitored continuously during the dip and pulled early if distressed. Fresh bottles only - H2O2 degrades once opened. Do NOT put back into a reef after a peroxide dip without a rinse.

tiers.recovery_instructions: At the end of the 30 minutes, move the fish into clean untreated seawater. Rinse the fish in clean seawater before returning to any display system — never put a peroxide-dipped fish directly back into a reef. Use only fresh, unopened 3% hydrogen peroxide; the concentration degrades after opening.
tiers.aeration_note: Hydrogen peroxide releases oxygen as it decays, so strict oxygenation aeration is not required, but follow the default bath-safety rule of providing aeration for any bath over one minute for circulation and monitoring.
tiers.abort_criteria_supplement: Noga specifically warns that many fish do not tolerate peroxide, and that overdose-related gill damage may take up to 24 hours to manifest as mortality. Monitor continuously during the bath and for at least a full day afterward.
tiers.indication: External ectoparasite dip for marine fish prior to introduction into a clean system.
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: Peroxide toxicity rises with water temperature and with smaller fish size. Bioassay a single individual first for any untested species.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Dose per Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide and the Humblefish peroxide thread. Supplemental toxicity, aeration, and monitoring-window data paraphrased from Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Hydrogen Peroxide entry, book p.401 (PDF p.420). Noga's own bath protocols for H2O2 use different concentrations and durations than Humblefish; per Jeff's adjudication of the discrepancy, the Humblefish concentration and duration are authoritative for this schema — see discrepancy-report.md item #2.

bath_protocol.calendar_event_note: Single-session dip; no .ics export per Jeff's calendar policy

medications.source_note: In-tank (bare QT) protocol with Kordon Methylene Blue (2.303% liquid): 1 tsp (5 ml) per 10 US gal — i.e. 0.5 ml/gal of 2.303% MB, which maintains approximately 3 ppm methylene blue. Dose every 48 hours for 10 days with a 25% water change before each dose. Remove carbon and switch off UV. MB stains silicone permanently — never use a bath container you plan to repurpose. Source: Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide, Methylene Blue entry.
medications.special_notes_generic: Humblefish prefers aquarium brand liquid (Kordon or Fritz). 30-min bath (preferred): 1 tsp (5 ml) of 2.303% MB per 5 gallons. QT protocol: 1 tsp per 10 gallons every other day for 10 days with water changes before each dose (~3 ppm maintained). Stains silicone and soft plastics; remove carbon and switch off UV.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 tsp (5 ml) per 10 US gal = 3 ppm MB (standard); 1 tsp per 5 gal for a 30-min bath
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Concentration on label is 2.303% methylene blue. 4 oz bottle treats ~240 gal. Staining is permanent on silicone - avoid display-tank use.

tiers.concentration_equivalent: 1 tsp of Kordon Methylene Blue per 5 gal
tiers.recovery_instructions: Return the fish to clean untreated seawater after 30 minutes. Methylene blue permanently stains silicone and soft plastics — never use a bath container you plan to repurpose. MB is also toxic to nitrifying bacteria, so carry-over into a biologically filtered display system can crash the filter.
tiers.indication: Short bath for bacterial and water-mold infections in marine fish, buffering against ammonia/nitrite stress, and adjunct oxygenation support during stressful procedures.
tiers.species_sensitivity_supplement: Noga documents MB only in freshwater contexts; saltwater bath safety data is limited. Approach scaleless and delicate species conservatively.
tiers.source_note: Noga (Noga, E.J. Fish Disease: Diagnosis and Treatment, 2nd edition, Wiley-Blackwell, 2010, Chapter 17 'Pharmacopoeia', Methylene Blue entry, book p.406, PDF p.425) documents MB only as a freshwater prolonged-immersion treatment for aquarium fish eggs (2 mg/L, alternate days, up to 3 total) and for freshwater ectoparasites (1–3 mg/L prolonged immersion). Noga gives no saltwater bath protocol for MB and specifically warns against prolonged-immersion use in any system with biological filtration. The saltwater bath concentration in this record is Humblefish-only per Jeff's adjudication — see discrepancy-report.md item #3.
tiers.verification_source_citation: Dose per Humblefish Medication Dosing Guide and the Humblefish Methylene Blue thread: 1 tsp (5 ml) of 2.303% MB per 5 gal, 30 minutes. Noga consulted for safety context only; does not provide a saltwater bath concentration.

medications.special_notes_generic: Water-column dosing: 12.5-25 mg/gal every 48 h (24 h for severe) with 25% WC before each dose; treat 10 days. Equally (more?) effective when fed: bind to food with Seachem Focus + Epsom salt + vitamins. Photolabile - light-shield the medicated water.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1-2 measures per 40 L (10 US gallons); each measure delivers ~100-125 mg metronidazole per Seachem's own description
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DailyMed confirms 70% metronidazole. One level measure weighs 150 mg (FisHotel in-house), delivering 105 mg metronidazole per measure. 1.19 measures per 10 gal hits Humblefish's 12.5 mg/gal floor; 2.4 measures per 10 gal reaches the 25 mg/gal ceiling. Matches Seachem's 1-2 measures per 10 gal label range.

brand_equivalents.brand_label_dose: 1 packet per 10 US gal (delivers 250 mg metronidazole + 75 mg praziquantel per 10 gal)
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Label dose delivers 25 mg/gal metronidazole - matches HF high end. Double-active means you're also dosing 7.5 mg/gal praziquantel whether you want it or not.

medications.special_notes_generic: Combination antibiotic containing Trimethoprim + Sulfathiazole sodium. Humblefish recommends 1/4 tsp per 10 gal daily × 10 days with 25% water change before each dose. TMP-Sulfa is synergistic against a broader spectrum than either drug alone. Remove invertebrates during treatment.

Broad-spectrum tetracycline-class antibiotic. Active in Fritz Maracyn Two. Label schedule: escalating packets over 5 days (1/1/2/2/2 packets per 10 gal). Effective against gram-positive and some gram-negative bacteria; often paired with Maracyn (erythromycin) for broader coverage. Remove invertebrates; will discolor water.

brand_equivalents.manufacturer_label_dose: 1 packet (10 mg minocycline) per 10 US gallons; 5-day escalating schedule: 1/1/2/2/2 packets
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: DailyMed confirms 10 mg minocycline per packet. Full packet weight not published by Fritz; recommend contacting Fritz Aquatics customer support for total packet weight.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: fda.report/DailyMed Maracyn Two: 0.6 g per packet (10 mg minocycline)

medications.special_notes_generic: Internal dewormer for round worms (Camallanus, Capillaria) and tapeworms. Dose 7.6 mg/gal once weekly × 3 weeks, OR food-soak with Seachem Focus as binder. Distinct from fenbendazole external bath (95 mg/gal × 12 h). Safe for biofilter; not reef-safe (harmful to corals, worms, hydrozoans).

brand_equivalents.manufacturer_label_dose: 1 g granule packets (off-label aquarium use); weigh 34 mg Panacur C per gallon for internal/once-weekly protocol
brand_equivalents.brand_notes: Off-label aquarium use. Always weigh on a mg scale. Food-soak with Focus for internal parasites.
brand_equivalents.measure_weight_source: Panacur C label states 1 g (1000 mg) per packet
